page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the KVM hypervisor and built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 24 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on external storage solutions, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations position this instance within the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng CPU performance of 1836.29 and a multi-core score of 23245.48. Memory bandwidth tests show 1267.06 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 128.31 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong performance in LLM text generation using a small 135M model, achieving 282.28 tokens per second, while medium 7B model text generation operates at an average rate of 15.15 tokens per second.

The lack of local storage requires users to attach network-attached block storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the workload. However, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a stable resource density for general-purpose workloads. This instance is suitable for web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate machine learning inference tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.6xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 24 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a PassMark CPU score of 28981.96 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 11454.0. Notably, it exhibits strong performance in text generation for small LLM models, reaching 282.28 tokens per second. Lacking local storage, it relies on network-attached volumes, making it suitable for balanced workloads such as web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate-scale inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.6xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 96 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced compute and memory performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring twenty-four dedicated Intel Xeon cores and ninety-six gigabytes of RAM for diverse enterprise workloads.

96.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
